channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mike Sonko and Raila Odinga Recognised at Heroes of Africa Awards for Service to Society
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko and the late former Prime Minister Raila Odinga were recognised at the Heroes of Africa Awards, which were held at the Kenyatta International Conference Centre (KICC...

item.title: SERAP Urges INEC to Investigate Alleged N800bn Misuse by APC Governors for Campaigns
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don call on the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to sharply investigate claims say some governors for the All Progressives ...

item.title: An Insight into the Princess of Wales’s Personal Expenses and Lifestyle
item.description: The Princess of Wales, Kate Middleton, maintains a carefully managed personal budget funded largely by profits from the Duchy of Cornwall, a private estate generating around £22 million annually. Whil...

item.title: Fremantle Dockers Secure Ninth Consecutive Win with Strong Performance Against Essendon at MCG
item.description: Fremantle, also known as Walyalup for Sir Doug Nicholls Round, secured a record-equalling ninth consecutive win with a dominant 104-61 victory over Essendon at the MCG. Pat Voss starred with five goal...

item.title: Celebrating the Life and Legacy of Silvia Pinal in Mexican Cinema
item.description: Silvia Pinal is one of the most influential figures in Latin American entertainment, recognized as the “Last Diva” of Mexico’s Golden Age of Cinema. Her career spans multiple decades and showcases rem...

item.title: Ramaphosa to Seek Court Interdict to Delay Phala Phala Impeachment Proceedings
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is preparing to challenge the impeachment process stemming from the Phala Phala scandal through legal channels. According to sources close to the president, he intends to see...

item.title: UK Announces Updates to the 1990 Computer Misuse Act to Protect Security Researchers and Strengthen Cybercrime Laws
item.description: The United Kingdom is moving forward with long-awaited reforms to the Computer Misuse Act of 1990, a law originally passed in response to a high-profile hacking incident involving the Duke of Edinburg...

item.title: Mets Pitcher Clay Holmes Out Indefinitely with Fibula Fracture After Yankees Game
item.description: Clay Holmes, one of the few consistent performers for the New York Mets this season, suffered a fractured right fibula after being hit by a comebacker from Spencer Jones during Friday's game against t...

item.title: Tennessee redistricting divides Memphis, weakening Black voters' political influence
item.description: Tennessee’s newly drawn congressional districts have fractured the city of Memphis, historically a Black-majority stronghold. The redistricting, driven by Republican lawmakers, splits the city across ...

item.title: India Takes Measures to Support Rupee Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: India is facing serious pressure on its rupee as oil prices surge due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. Since February, the rupee has fallen over 5%, continuing losses from last year, making it Asi...

item.title: Cubana Chief Priest loses APC nomination for Imo Reps seat
item.description: Naija socialite and businessman, Pascal “Cubana Chief Priest” Okechukwu, never manage to carry the All Progressives Congress (APC) ticket for Imo State House of Representatives seat. Him contest for t...

item.title: Pakistan, Turkiye, and Azerbaijan Enhance Trilateral Cooperation, Says President Aliyev
item.description: President Ilham Aliyev of Azerbaijan met with Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz in Baku, highlighting that Azerbaijan-Pakistan relations are being strengthened within a trilateral framework including...

item.title: Matt Brittin Appointed BBC Director-General After Consulting Gordon Brown
item.description: Matt Brittin has been appointed as the new director-general of the BBC, succeeding Tim Davie. Brittin, 57, previously served as president of Google in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa for a decade,...

item.title: Understanding the Global Risks and Impacts of Nuclear War
item.description: Amid rising geopolitical tensions, the potential consequences of nuclear conflict have regained urgent attention. The Bulletin explores the renewed study of nuclear war and its global environmental ef...

item.title: Hulk Hogan Escorts Cyndi Lauper at the 1985 Grammy Awards
item.description: At the 27th Annual Grammy Awards on February 26, 1985, an unforgettable moment occurred when Cyndi Lauper was accompanied by WWF Champion Hulk Hogan, who acted as her bodyguard for the evening. In a s...

item.title: Ms Hills seeks strategic partners to grow her luxury brand GemsByHills after 13 years of success
item.description: For over 13 years, Ugwu Nkechinyere Obumneme, better known as Ms Hills, don dey run CMHairByHills, one premium hair brand wey don gain respect for consistency, quality, and trust for Naija. Unlike man...

item.title: UK Eurovision entrant Sam Battle reacts graciously after finishing last with minimal points
item.description: Sam Battle, known as Look Mum No Computer, represented the United Kingdom in the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 with his song 'Eins, Zwei, Drei'. Despite his efforts, the UK finished last, receiving zer...

item.title: FIA arrests two in Karachi for posing as government officials using fake documents
item.description: In Karachi, the Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) Anti-Corruption Circle has detained two persons, including a woman, for impersonating government officials. The suspects claimed to represent the so-...

item.title: Experts Advise Safe Relocation of Snakes in Islamabad Amid Increased Sightings
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Residents of Islamabad have been noticing snakes more often in various parts of the city, which has raised some concern. Wildlife experts emphasise that killing snakes is unnecessary and po...

item.title: NEET-UG 2026 Paper Leak: CBI Probes NTA Insiders and Multi-State Middlemen Network
item.description: The NEET-UG 2026 examination paper leak scandal has created major shockwaves across India, forcing the authorities to schedule a re-examination on 21st June for over 2.2 million aspirants. According t...
